Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using

input.initialPrompt:
I have an ObservableCollection<Product> with Name and Price properties.
Show me how to display these products in a vertical scrollable list using
CollectionView with a DataTemplate that shows both the name and price.
Use compiled bindings.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ProductsPage.xaml):**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:models="clr-namespace:YourApp.Models"
             x:Class="YourApp.ProductsPage"
             x:DataType="local:ProductsViewModel">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Products}"
                    ItemsLayout="VerticalList">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Product">
                <HorizontalStackLayout Padding="10" Spacing="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}"
                           FontSize="18"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                    <Label Text="{Binding Price, StringFormat='{0:C}'}"
                           FontSize="16"
                           TextColor="Gray"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </HorizontalStackLayout>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

**Key points:**

- **`x:DataType`** on the page targets your ViewModel; on the `DataTemplate` it targets `Product` — this enables **compiled bindings** (resolved at compile time, not reflection).
- **`ItemsLayout="VerticalList"`** is the default, but shown explicitly for clarity.
- **`StringFormat='{0:C}'`** formats the price as currency.

**Model & ViewModel:**
```csharp
public class Product
{
    public string Name { get; set; }
    public decimal Price { get; set; }
}

public partial class ProductsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    public ObservableCollection<Product> Products { get; } = new()
    {
        new Product { Name = "Widget", Price = 9.99m },
        new Product { Name = "Gadget", Price = 24.95m },
    };
}
```

Set the `BindingContext` in code-behind or via DI:
```csharp
public ProductsPage(ProductsViewModel vm)
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = vm;
}
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) XAML example using CollectionView with compiled bindings.
